title: Panda3D SceneGraphArenaHard
summary: A two-phase benchmark: first repair the hollowed Panda3D pgraph engine (TransformState math/calculations/diagnostics, RenderState compose/diagnostics, PandaNode scene operations, NodePath transforms/scene-utils/relative-transform), then implement the three headless Python game scripts that build a 12-node scene graph, run 27 ticks of operations, and write deterministic output files.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A Panda3D engine source tree at /workspace/panda3d - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (SceneGraphArenaHard) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A Panda3D engine source tree at /workspace/panda3d
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (SceneGraphArenaHard)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

SceneGraphArenaHard builds a 12-node multi-branch scene graph and exercises
reparenting, orientation, color scaling, billboarding, instancing, flattening,
and bounds computation across 27 ticks of operations. The game records a JSONL
event stream and a JSON final state with a deterministic SHA-256 hash.

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Complete the intentionally hollowed pgraph engine code. Large sections of
   TransformState composition/decomposition, RenderState composition, PandaNode
   scene operations, and NodePath transform/scene-utility methods have been
   removed or reduced to stubs that compile but produce incorrect results.
   You must retrieve code context across the pgraph module and restore correct
   behavior end-to-end.
2) Build the Panda3D SDK from source:
     /workspace/scripts/build_panda3d.sh
3) Run the engine's focused scene-graph regression tests:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh
   All tests must pass with zero failures. Fix any issues you find.

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) The game scripts under /workspace/game_project/scripts are stubs. Implement them
   so SceneGraphArenaHard correctly:
   - Creates a headless ShowBase scene with a 12-node multi-branch tree
   - Executes 27 ticks of scene graph operations (position queries, reparenting,
     orientation, color scaling, billboarding, instancing, flattening, bounds,
     stashing, and more)
   - Records events, scores, and aggregate fields in canonical JSON
   The game is roughly 750 lines of Python across the three project scripts.
5) Run the game twice with the same replay and seed, verify determinism:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy the first run's output as the canonical result:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Build the determinism report:
     Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with fields:
       same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash, run_1_event_lines,
       run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace.)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/pgraph_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/pgraph_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- Do not stub out engine logic; every function must have a correct implementation.
- The transform composition, NodePath relative transform, and all scene graph
  operation subsystems must be fully functional, not approximated or bypassed.

moduleDag.description: A two-phase benchmark: first repair the hollowed Panda3D pgraph engine (TransformState math/calculations/diagnostics, RenderState compose/diagnostics, PandaNode scene operations, NodePath transforms/scene-utils/relative-transform), then implement the three headless Python game scripts that build a 12-node scene graph, run 27 ticks of operations, and write deterministic output files.

nodes.label: TransformState Composition Math
nodes.description: Restores factory methods (make_pos_hpr_scale_shear, make_pos_quat_scale_shear, make_mat, make_mat3) that construct TransformState objects from position, HPR, scale, and shear components. These were stubbed to return make_identity().

nodes.label: RenderState Compose Pipeline
nodes.description: Restores the RenderState composition pipeline: compose(), invert_compose(), do_compose(), and do_invert_compose(). These were stubbed to return other or make_empty(), breaking render attribute propagation through the scene graph.

nodes.label: TransformState Calculations
nodes.description: Restores internal decomposition routines: do_calc_mat, do_calc_components, do_calc_hpr, calc_quat, calc_norm_quat, and calc_singular. These were stubbed to return identity/zero values, breaking HPR extraction and matrix decomposition.

nodes.label: RenderState Diagnostics
nodes.description: Restores RenderState diagnostic/introspection functions: output(), validate_filled_slots(), store_into_cache(), do_validate_composition_cache(), do_hash_key(), and write_datagram(). These were stubbed to emit placeholder text or return 0.

nodes.label: Replay I/O Utilities
nodes.description: Implements load_replay() with full tree+tick validation, canonicalize_value() for precision-normalized JSON, and write_json() for reproducible SHA-256 hashes. Pure Python utilities with no Panda3D dependency.

nodes.label: TransformState Diagnostics
nodes.description: Restores TransformState diagnostic functions: compare_to, operator==, calc_hash, and validate_composition_cache. These traverse the composition cache and compare transform states; they are only meaningful once the core math has been restored.

nodes.label: PandaNode Scene Operations
nodes.description: Restores PandaNode methods for bounds computation (get_internal_bounds, compute_external_bounds), transform/state propagation (set_transform, set_state), node copying (r_copy_subgraph, r_copy_children), child management (stage_remove_child, stage_replace_child), and flatten support (combine_with).

nodes.label: NodePath Relative Transform Fix
nodes.description: Fixes the operand-order bug in get_relative_point and get_relative_vector. The fix can only be validated once invert_compose() returns correct results.

nodes.label: NodePath Transform Resolution
nodes.description: Restores NodePath cross-node transform queries and mutations: get_transform(other), get_state(other), set_pos(other), set_hpr(other), set_fluid_pos(other), r_get_net_transform, r_get_partial_transform. All stubbed to return identity.

nodes.label: NodePath Scene Utilities
nodes.description: Restores high-level NodePath operations: find/find_all_matches, instance_to/ copy_to, wrt_reparent_to, look_at/heads_up, set_color_scale/compose_color_scale, billboard effects, flatten_medium/flatten_strong, calc_tight_bounds, stash/unstash, set_light, and texture/material queries. All stubbed to return empty or no-op.

nodes.label: SceneGraphArenaHard Game Logic
nodes.description: Implements run_arena(): spins up a headless ShowBase, builds a 12-node tree from replay data, then executes 27 ticks of scene graph operations (position queries, reparenting, orientation, color scaling, billboarding, instancing, flattening, bounds, stashing). Exercises the full repaired transform pipeline end-to-end.

nodes.label: Replay Runner CLI
nodes.description: CLI entry point that parses arguments, loads the replay via replay_io, calls run_arena(), validates results, summarizes events, and writes final_state.json, pgraph_events.jsonl, and engine_trace.json. Depends on both replay_io and scene_graph_arena being complete.

edges.label: Diagnostic functions compare transforms and traverse the composition cache; meaningful only after math is correct
Diagnostics need correct HPR/quat decomposition to validate transform states
Relative transform fix calls invert_compose(); needs correct transform math
NodePath transform resolution needs correct matrix decomposition
Scene utilities (color_scale, lights) rely on RenderState composition
NodePath scene utils call PandaNode methods (combine_with, bounds, copy)
Scene utilities (wrt_reparent_to, look_at) use relative transform resolution
Arena queries relative positions via get_relative_point/vector
Arena exercises all scene utilities (find, flatten, billboard, etc.)
replay_runner imports load_replay() and write_json() from replay_io
